description: A Department of Labor designed graphic combining a desaturated black-and-white rally photograph with a slogan headline and a quoted news card. The top photo shows a crowd of cheering blue-collar workers at what appears to be a U.S. Steel ('UsS / United States Steel') event: men in hard hats (one bearded older man in a hard hat smiling broadly center-frame), some wearing 'MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN' caps, and one holding a printed sign reading 'THANK YOU PRESIDENT TRUMP!' with a U.S. Steel logo and small American flag. A dark navy gradient overlays the lower portion. A large bold white serif headline reads 'PROTECTING AMERICANS.' Below is a white rounded text card headed by the 'Bloomberg Law' logo with the quote: 'The Labor Department assessed nearly $318 million in back pay and penalties from employers accused of minimum wage, overtime, and child labor violations in fiscal year 2025, a 33% increase from the year before, according to agency data. The monetary penalties are the highest recorded by the DOL's Wage and Hour Division in the last decade, and the most back wages since 2019.' A small DOL seal sits at the bottom. Propaganda technique: pairing enthusiastic worker imagery and pro-Trump signage with a cited statistic from a credible outlet to portray the administration as a vigorous protector of American workers. Target audience: blue-collar workers, unions, and the administration's base.
notable_text: PROTECTING AMERICANS / THANK YOU PRESIDENT TRUMP! / nearly $318 million in back pay and penalties ... a 33% increase from the year before
